data.task:
Ticket 0023 — atom 0 of 2: give the ONE archive owner (`requestAuthoringPlay` in packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts) a reachable out-of-run CLI surface. Do NOT add a second raw archive path or any direct file-move; every byte of behavior must flow through the existing `requestAuthoringPlay`. 

OWNER MAP (read these before changing anything): `requestAuthoringPlay(ctx, {workspaceId, persona, playId, invocation})` already exists and is the sole authoring dispatcher (launcher.ts ~line 930). It is currently invoked ONLY in-process from packages/daemon/src/oz-chat.ts (the `author` command) / oz-host.ts. There is NO HTTP route for it and NO `cocoder` CLI verb. The CLI verbs live in packages/cli/src/run.ts; the loopback HTTP client helpers live in packages/cli/src/client.ts; daemon HTTP routes live in packages/daemon/src/routes.ts. Mirror the EXISTING patterns in those files exactly (auth bearer + x-oz-csrf-token, op import from launcher, sendJson status mapping).

DO (three surfaces + tests, all wired to the one owner):
1. packages/daemon/src/routes.ts — add a new route `POST /workspaces/:id/author` that reads JSON body {persona, playId, invocation}, calls `requestAuthoringPlay(ctx, {workspaceId: <:id>, persona, playId, invocation})`, and returns its LaunchResult via the same sendJson(status, body) pattern the other routes use. Default `persona` to 'oscar' if absent (this surface is the founder/Oscar out-of-run path). Follow the auth/CSRF guard that the other mutation routes in this file already apply.
2. packages/cli/src/client.ts — add `authorViaDaemon(baseUrl, workspaceId, playId, invocation)` mirroring `supportCommitViaDaemon`/`migrateHistoryViaDaemon` (fetch /auth/session, POST with bearer+CSRF+content-type json, body JSON.stringify({persona:'oscar', playId, invocation}), throw on !res.ok, return parsed json). Export a result type if the sibling helpers do.
3. packages/cli/src/run.ts — add a `cocoder oz author <playId> [--json '<invocation-json>']` verb BEFORE the final usage fallthrough. Parse playId as the 4th argv; read invocation from a `--json` flag (JSON.parse; default {} if absent). Probe the daemon (reuse the existing probeDaemon pattern); if not alive, error like the other verbs. Call authorViaDaemon and print the committed sha / paths (mirror commit-support's output handling). Append the new verb to BOTH usage strings in this file (the early one and the line ~95 fallthrough), so `cocoder` with no/!match args lists it.
4. Tests: add a daemon route test (packages/daemon/tests/) proving `POST /workspaces/cocoder/author` dispatches to requestAuthoringPlay and surfaces its status/body (assert it reaches the op — e.g. with a stub/fake ctx like authoring-play.test.ts uses, or via the route handler unit). Cover: happy dispatch (200-ish passthrough), unknown workspace passthrough (404), and that an invalid playId returns the op's 400. Reuse existing fixtures/patterns; do not invent a new harness.

ACCEPTANCE: `pnpm -w typecheck` (or the repo's typecheck) is clean; the new + all existing daemon/cli tests pass (`pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` and `@cocoder/cli` if it has tests); `cocoder oz author archive-priority --json '{"id":"x"}'` is now a recognized verb that routes to requestAuthoringPlay (you do not need to run it live — the test proves the wiring). 

MUST NOT BREAK: the existing oz-chat/oz-host `author` in-process path; existing authoring-play.test.ts; the support-commit archive 409 guard (leave its message alone — atom 1 updates it). Do NOT add a raw archive file-move anywhere. WRITE-SCOPE: packages/daemon/src/routes.ts, packages/cli/src/client.ts, packages/cli/src/run.ts, and test files under packages/daemon/tests/ (and packages/cli/tests/ if that dir exists). Match surrounding style; touch nothing else.

data.reason: Out-of-scope: archived a priority file (orchestration-audit-and-refactor.md -> archive/, order.json) never asked for; and added a duplicate authoring path (authorViaDaemon + /author route) alongside the existing authoringPlayViaDaemon + /authoring-plays route, worsening the very duplication this priority targets instead of one owner.

data.task:
Ticket 0023 — the REAL fix (atom 1 of 2): consolidate to exactly ONE authoring HTTP path, name the reachable verb in the support-commit refusal, and add an enforcer that fails if a second authoring path ever reappears. Scope is small and surgical — do NOT archive any priority, do NOT add new client helpers or verbs, do NOT touch the live path's behavior.

BACKGROUND (already verified by Oscar — trust this owner map, but confirm before deleting): HEAD has a DUPLICATE authoring HTTP surface. The LIVE owner is `POST /workspaces/:id/authoring-plays/:playId` (packages/daemon/src/routes.ts ~line 925, parser `authoringPlayBody`) — it is what the CLI client `authoringPlayViaDaemon` (packages/cli/src/client.ts ~line 78, posts to `/authoring-plays/${playId}`), BOTH `cocoder oz` verbs (run.ts: `archive-priority` and `author`), and every daemon/cli test already use. The ORPHAN is `POST /workspaces/:id/author` (routes.ts ~line 909, parser `authorBody` ~line 253, type `ParsedAuthorBody` ~line 154) — grep proves NOTHING calls it (no client, no dashboard, no test). Both routes just call the one in-process owner `requestAuthoringPlay`; the oz-chat/oz-host in-process `author` tool calls `requestAuthoringPlay` directly and does NOT use either HTTP route, so removing the orphan route cannot affect it.

DO:
1. packages/daemon/src/routes.ts — DELETE the orphan `/author` route block (the `method==='POST' && seg.length===3 && seg[2]==='author'` branch, ~909-923), and DELETE the now-unused `authorBody` function (~253) and the `ParsedAuthorBody` type (~154). FIRST grep the whole repo to confirm `authorBody` / `ParsedAuthorBody` / the `/author` (seg3) route have no other references; if anything else uses them, STOP and report instead of deleting. Leave the `/authoring-plays/:playId` route, `authoringPlayBody`, and `ParsedAuthoringPlayBody` completely untouched.
2. packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts — in the post-wrap support-commit archive refusal (~line 715, the 409 whose message currently ends 'use the archive-priority authoring Play after an archive-ready founder confirmation'), extend the message to NAME the now-reachable out-of-run verb, e.g. append: ' — run `cocoder oz archive-priority <priorityId>` (the one archive-priority Play; no raw file move).' Keep it one clear sentence; do not change the 409 status, the refusedPaths, or the guard logic.
3. ENFORCER (the ticket's 'automated check must fail when a second authoring path appears'): add a test (extend packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts, near the existing authoring-plays tests, or a small new test file) with TWO assertions: (a) BEHAVIORAL — `POST /workspaces/cocoder/author` now returns 404 (orphan path gone) while `POST /workspaces/cocoder/authoring-plays/archive-priority` still dispatches through requestAuthoringPlay (reuse the existing fixture/stub pattern from the current authoring-plays tests in that file); (b) SOURCE-LEVEL single-owner pin — read packages/daemon/src/routes.ts as text and assert it contains exactly ONE authoring-play dispatch route (e.g. exactly one occurrence of `seg[2] === 'authoring-plays'` AND zero occurrences of `seg[2] === 'author'`), so a future second route makes the test fail. Add a one-line comment tying it to ticket 0023.

ACCEPTANCE: repo typecheck clean; `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` and `pnpm --filter @cocoder/cli test` both green (the existing `/authoring-plays/:playId` tests must STILL pass unchanged — they prove the live owner is intact); the new enforcer passes and would fail if either a `/author` route or a second authoring dispatch is reintroduced; `git grep -n "authorBody\|ParsedAuthorBody"` returns nothing.

MUST NOT BREAK: the live `/authoring-plays/:playId` route and its tests; `authoringPlayViaDaemon`; both `cocoder oz` verbs; the in-process oz-chat/oz-host `author` tool. WRITE-SCOPE: packages/daemon/src/routes.ts, packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts (only the 409 message string), and test files under packages/daemon/tests/. Touch nothing else; match surrounding style.

data.reason: Orphan /author route + authorBody/ParsedAuthorBody removed (one authoring owner), 409 names `cocoder oz archive-priority`, enforcer pins single path; typecheck clean, daemon 236/236 + cli 4/4 green, git grep authorBody clean in src.

data.outcome: ✅ COMMITTED on `main` — 2 commit(s) on the active branch (no landing step; work is on the branch by construction). Nothing out of lane.
